Package party.iroiro.luajava.value

Interface LuaValue

  • All Superinterfaces:
    java.lang.AutoCloseable
    All Known Implementing Classes:
    AbstractLuaValue, ImmutableLuaValue, RefLuaValue
    public interface LuaValue
extends java.lang.AutoCloseable
    A simple wrapper of references to Lua values

    You will need to manually close values of complex types. See close().

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods Deprecated Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method Description
      @Nullable LuaValue[] call​(java.lang.Object... parameters)
      Performs thisLuaValue(parameter1, parameter2, ...)
      void close()
      Deprecated.
      the value now clears Lua-side references on finalization
      LuaValue get​(int i)  
      LuaValue get​(java.lang.String key)  
      LuaValue get​(LuaValue key)  
      void push()
      Pushes the Lua value onto the Lua stack
      void push​(Lua L)
      Pushes the Lua value onto the Lua stack of another thread sharing a same main state
      void set​(int i, LuaValue value)
      Performs thisLuaValue[i] = value
      void set​(java.lang.String key, LuaValue value)
      Performs thisLuaValue[key] = value
      void set​(LuaValue key, LuaValue value)
      Performs thisLuaValue[key] = value
      Lua state()  
      @Nullable java.lang.Object toJavaObject()  
      Lua.LuaType type()  

    • Method Detail

      • type

        Returns:
        the type of the Lua value

        Pushes the Lua value onto the Lua stack

        void push​(Lua L)
        Pushes the Lua value onto the Lua stack of another thread sharing a same main state

        L - another thread

      • set

        void set​(java.lang.String key,
         LuaValue value)
        Performs thisLuaValue[key] = value

        value - the value

      • set

        void set​(LuaValue key,
         LuaValue value)
        Performs thisLuaValue[key] = value
        Parameters:
        key - the key
        value - the value

      • call

        @Nullable
@Nullable LuaValue[] call​(java.lang.Object... parameters)
        Performs thisLuaValue(parameter1, parameter2, ...)
        Parameters:
        parameters - the parameters
        Returns:
        the return values, null on error

      • toJavaObject

        @Nullable
@Nullable java.lang.Object toJavaObject()
        Returns:
        a Java value converted from this Lua value
        See Also:
        Lua.toObject(int)

      • close

        @Deprecated
void close()
        Deprecated.
        the value now clears Lua-side references on finalization
        Does nothing
        Specified by:
        close in interface java.lang.AutoCloseable
